1.What went well: In the design process, I redesigned the low fidelity bottom side menu and added a gamification feature based on feedback from the previous presentation. Before working on the gamification feature, I researched some basic information about gamification. I found it interesting because it helps businesses engage users and motivates people to achieve their goals. I also found UX writing interesting during last Thursday's class, as it can help simplify UI design and improve the user experience.
2. What didn’t go well: Designing the badges was challenging because I do not have a graphic design background. It was difficult to create badge designs, so I decided to keep them simple by using the Pen tool in Figma to draw basic shapes and lines instead of creating complex designs.
3. What can be improved: I would like to improve my UI design layout and make it more visually appealing and user friendly.
4. What actions will I take for the next: I learned that design improvements often require multiple iterations based on feedback. During this sprint, I redesigned parts of my low fidelity prototype because some design elements needed to be changed. For the next sprint, I will continue refining my designs, seek more feedback, and focus on improving the overall user experience and visual consistency.

1.What did I accomplish yesterday or las: Last Friday, I added a gamification page to my design. I found the process interesting because it allowed me to explore new ways to engage and motivate users.
2.What will I do today?: Today, I will continue developing the gamification page and explore additional features and content that could improve the user experience.
3.Are there any blockers?: One challenge I am facing is generating new ideas for the gamification page. I am still exploring different approaches and features that could add value to the design.
